name: Poblano Rice (Bayless)
recipeIngredient: 2 poblano chilies, stems and seeds removed -- roughly chopped
12 sprigs cilantro, plus extra -- for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 2-quart saucepan, combine broth and chilies. Bring to boil, partially cover and simmer about 10 minutes, until chilies are very soft.
2. Pour into food processor, add cilantro and process to smooth puree. Press through medium-mesh strainer into bowl and stir in salt.
3. Wipe pan clean, add oil. Over medium heat cook rice and onion, stirring regularly, until rice is chalky looking and onion soft, about 5 minutes.
4. Stir in garlic and cook 1 minute longer. Add warm or reheated chili liquid to rice, stir once, scrape down rice kernels clinging to side of pan, cover and cook over medium-low heat 15 minutes.
5. Uncover and check rice. It should be nearly cooked through. If so, turn off heat, re-cover and let stand 5 to 10 minutes longer to complete cooking. Fluff with fork, scoop into warm serving dish, decorate with cilantro and serve.
